title: Noshing and Moshing

jokes.text: WOMEN LOVE THIS. THEY DON'T CARE THAT RERUN'S FAT 'CAUSE HE'S GOT THE MOVES.
jokes.setup: Bill defending his seizure-like dancing moves
jokes.punchline: Invoking Rerun from What's Happening as his dance inspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bill character moment - his earnest belief in terrible dance moves and pop culture references

jokes.text: YOU CUT ME OFF MID-FUNK.
jokes.setup: Music stops while Bill is dancing
jokes.punchline: Bill's serious complaint about being interrupted during his 'funk'
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character specificity - only Bill would use 'mid-funk' seriously

jokes.text: IF I HAD, I'D BE FIGHTING 'EM OFF WITH A STICK.
jokes.setup: Bill explaining he hasn't shown his dance moves to ladies yet
jokes.punchline: His delusional confidence about the inevitable result
jokes.explanation: Classic delusional geek confidence trope, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: IT'LL BRING US POWER, RESPECT, AND MONEY, AND IT'S RIGHT HERE IN THE SUITCASE.
jokes.setup: Neal building dramatic tension about his life-changing discovery
jokes.punchline: The grandiose promises for what turns out to be a ventriloquist dummy
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup for the ventriloquist dummy reveal

jokes.text: HI, DUMMIES.
jokes.setup: Neal introduces the ventriloquist dummy to his friends
jokes.punchline: The dummy immediately insults them as 'dummies'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective pun on dummy/dummies, economical

jokes.text: YEAH, IF YOU LIKE CLOWNS. CLOWNS.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether Jenna is still hot
jokes.punchline: Ken's dismissive 'clowns' comment about her punk look
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited joke that fits Ken's character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: WHAT, DID YOU EVEN GET YOUR NAME RIGHT AT LEAST?
jokes.setup: Neal got a D on his history test
jokes.punchline: Harold's sarcastic escalation about basic competence
jokes.explanation: Classic dad joke response, fits Harold's character perfectly

jokes.text: WILLIE TYLER AND LESTER ARE ON THE HOLLYWOOD SQUARES THIS WEEK. I GOTTA WATCH.
jokes.setup: Neal asking to be excused from family discussion
jokes.punchline: His priority is watching a ventriloquist on a game show
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail showing Neal's new obsession bleeding into everything

jokes.text: WHAT KIND OF DOCTOR INVITES HIS PATIENTS OVER TO LISTEN TO HIS DENTAL JOKES? IT'S SICK.
jokes.setup: Harold complaining about the dentist party
jokes.punchline: His genuine disgust at the concept of dental jokes
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the awkwardness of professional social mixing

jokes.text: HEY, IT'S CALLED A FIGURE, NOT A DUMMY. A DUMMY IS SOMEONE WHO CALLS IT ONE.
jokes.setup: Bill calls the ventriloquist dummy a dummy
jokes.punchline: Neal's defensive wordplay correction
jokes.explanation: Good defensive wordplay that shows Neal's taking this seriously

jokes.text: BET YOU WANT A PIECE OF ME, HUH, STRETCH?
jokes.setup: Bill is intimidated by the dummy
jokes.punchline: The dummy challenging the tall, skinny Bill to a fight
jokes.explanation: Standard ventriloquist dummy aggressive behavior, economical delivery

jokes.text: PERHAPS YOU'VE NEVER SEEN A LITTLE SHOW CALLED SOAP. I MEAN, THAT GUY'S HILARIOUS. NOW HE'S RICH, THANKS TO VENTRILOQUISM.
jokes.setup: Friends dismiss ventriloquism as unfunny
jokes.punchline: Neal's earnest defense citing a specific TV show
jokes.explanation: Shows Neal's delusion through specific pop culture reference, good character work

jokes.text: YOU'RE 18. YOU'RE GOING TO BE THE OLDEST JUNIOR IN MICHIGAN.
jokes.setup: Daniel complaining about missing school
jokes.punchline: His mother's harsh reality check about his academic status
jokes.explanation: Brutally effective character moment highlighting Daniel's academic failure

jokes.text: I CAN BRING HOME A BIG WHOPPING 2.50 AN HOUR. YOU KNOW I WOULDN'T TURN THAT DOWN.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Desario suggesting Daniel should drop out to work
jokes.punchline: Daniel's sarcastic response about minimum wage
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic response showing Daniel's awareness of his limited options

jokes.text: WELL, YOU'RE CERTAINLY RELIABLE. YOU'RE ALWAYS A BITCH.
jokes.setup: Lindsay calling Daniel unreliable
jokes.punchline: Daniel's cruel but consistent comeback
jokes.explanation: Mean but structurally sound comeback, fits the relationship dynamics

jokes.text: IT'S CALLED WEDNESDAY.
jokes.setup: Family argument about responsibilities
jokes.punchline: Daniel's deadpan statement of the obvious day
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery highlighting the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: I DIDN'T WANT TO WRITE IT DOWN BECAUSE I DIDN'T WANT TO KILL A TREE. DID YOU KNOW THAT OUR RAINFORESTS ARE DISAPPEARING?
jokes.setup: Neal explaining why his report isn't written
jokes.punchline: Escalating to environmental activism as an excuse
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Neal's increasing desperation and creativity in excuse-making

jokes.text: YOU MEAN TO SEE JERRY? I LOVE THAT GUY. HE OWES ME MONEY!
jokes.setup: Being sent to the principal's office
jokes.punchline: Neal's casual familiarity and financial claim
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Neal's complete breakdown of social norms

jokes.text: [WHISPERING] He's always trying to sleep.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Desario saying father is trying to sleep
jokes.punchline: Daniel's whispered, exasperated aside about chronic behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Daniel's frustration with family dynamics

jokes.text: YOU'RE MANUFACTURING IDIOTS. WHAT KIND OF SYSTEM IS THIS?
jokes.setup: Lindsay arguing about detention rules
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic condemnation of the education system
jokes.explanation: Good Lindsay character moment showing her intellectual rebellion

jokes.text: THIS FOOD IS FIT FOR A KING. HERE, KING. HERE, KING.
jokes.setup: Standard ventriloquist comment about food quality
jokes.punchline: Calling for a dog named King to eat the bad food
jokes.explanation: Classic old-fashioned joke structure, well-delivered but predictable

jokes.text: HEY, SEIDELMAN, NICE HEAD. DID IT COME WITH A NECK?
jokes.setup: Bully mocking Neal's 'doll'
jokes.punchline: Morty's comeback about the bully's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good comeback structure with economical delivery, satisfying reversal

jokes.text: WHY DON'T YOU GO PICK ON SOMEONE YOUR OWN SIZE? THERE'S A BUS IN THE PARKING LOT.
jokes.setup: Standard 'pick on someone your own size' line
jokes.punchline: Suggesting the bully fight a bus
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of classic line with absurd escalation

jokes.text: IT'S SCHWEIBER. YOU TELL 'EM, MORTY.
jokes.setup: Neal correcting pronunciation of his name
jokes.punchline: His pride in the dummy's defense
jokes.explanation: Shows Neal's misplaced pride and dependency on the dummy

jokes.text: HEY, SCHMUCKO, WHY DON'T YOU GO KILL BILL?
jokes.setup: Sam referencing the movie Magic about evil dummies
jokes.punchline: Quoting what an evil dummy might say to Neal
jokes.explanation: Good meta reference showing Sam's movie knowledge and concerns about Neal

jokes.text: WHAT ARE YOU DOIN'? AREN'T YOU THE GUY THAT HAD THAT PSYCHO GIRLFRIEND?
jokes.setup: Daniel approaching Jenna at the record store
jokes.punchline: Her blunt reference to Lindsay as 'psycho girlfriend'
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Daniel's relationship drama is public knowledge

jokes.text: YOU KNOW WHAT PUNKERS DON'T DO? CALL THEMSELVES PUNKERS.
jokes.setup: Daniel claiming to be a punker
jokes.punchline: Jenna's gatekeeping correction about proper punk terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent gatekeeping joke with perfect structure and economy

jokes.text: OR WOULD THEY JUST RUN OUT NAKED? I DON'T KNOW.
jokes.setup: Bill wondering about pulling fire alarm during girls' PE
jokes.punchline: Sam's uncomfortable non-answer to inappropriate question
jokes.explanation: Typical teenage boy humor but shows Bill's inappropriate fixations

jokes.text: WOULD ALL THE GIRLS GET DRESSED BEFORE THEY RAN OUT OF THE LOCKER ROOM? OR WOULD THEY JUST RUN OUT NAKED?
jokes.setup: Discussion about pulling fire alarm
jokes.punchline: Awkward teenage boy fantasy question
jokes.explanation: Typical teenage boy humor, realistic but not particularly clever or funny

jokes.text: HELP ME! [GURGLES]
jokes.setup: Neal describing his lunch lady routine
jokes.punchline: The dummy pretending to drown in beans with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy bit, appropriately silly for the character

jokes.text: DAMN. WHAT IS GOING ON WITH THE WEIRS LATELY? YOU ALL LOOK SO HOT.
jokes.setup: Barry seeing Lindsay arrive
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate comment about the whole family
jokes.explanation: Appropriately creepy college guy behavior, uncomfortable but realistic

jokes.text: HEY, FAT ASS, WHY DON'T YOU SHOVE IT? [LAUGHING]
jokes.setup: Mr. Kowchevski telling students to get to class
jokes.punchline: Barry's oblivious rudeness followed by laughter
jokes.explanation: Shows Barry's obnoxious college attitude, gets reaction through shock value

jokes.text: THEY MAKE YOU PICK UP A MEATBALL WITH YOUR REAR END WALK IT TO THE OTHER SIDE OF THE ROOM. AND IF YOU DROP IT ON THE WAY, THEY MAKE YOU PICK IT UP AND EAT IT.
jokes.setup: Discussion about fraternity hazing
jokes.punchline: Barry's graphic description of supposed hazing ritual
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's absurd enough to be funny, if not sophisticated

jokes.text: WELL, I'M LEANING TOWARDS UNDECLARED. WITH A MINOR IN SITTING ON YOUR ASS.
jokes.setup: Barry discussing his college major
jokes.punchline: Harold's sarcastic addition about Barry's work ethic
jokes.explanation: Good dad joke at son's expense, fits both characters well

jokes.text: NOT REALLY, BUT LOOK HOW EXCITED THEY GET WHEN I SAY IT. UNBELIEVABLE.
jokes.setup: Barry mentioning pre-law as a major
jokes.punchline: His cynical observation about parental reactions
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on parent-child dynamics and expectations

jokes.text: BESIDES, YOU'RE A SCHWEIBER MAN! IRRESISTIBLE TO THE LADIES.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Barry playing the field
jokes.punchline: Harold's proud but ironic family boast
jokes.explanation: Ironic given Harold's own marital problems, adds dramatic irony layer

jokes.text: KISS FOR YOU? NO, NOT FOR ME. CLOSE THE DOOR. OK? IT'S ALL RIGHT.
jokes.setup: Neal practicing ventriloquist routine in bedroom
jokes.punchline: His awkward Sen̄or Wences impression
jokes.explanation: Reference to classic ventriloquist act, more cringe than funny

jokes.text: THAT YEAR, HE BOUGHT ME A CAR FOR MY BIRTHDAY.
jokes.setup: Barry explaining how he knew about dad's cheating
jokes.punchline: The implication that the car was hush money
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect economy, shows family dysfunction through understated implication

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, ALL THESE PUNK GIRLS HATE THEMSELVES. IT'S GOT TO IMPROVE YOUR CHANCES.
jokes.setup: Nick asking about chances at punk club
jokes.punchline: Ken's cynical assessment of punk girl psychology
jokes.explanation: Dark but insightful character observation, fits Ken's cynical worldview

jokes.text: HEY, UH, EDGAR WINTER CALLED. HE SAYS HE NEEDS HIS HAIR BACK FOR A GIG TONIGHT.
jokes.setup: Mocking Nick's bleached hair
jokes.punchline: Reference to albino rock musician Edgar Winter
jokes.explanation: Good reference joke that requires music knowledge, fits the era

jokes.text: OH, HERE IT COMES, 'DAD, CAN I BORROW SOME MONEY?'
jokes.setup: Lindsay approaching parents before party
jokes.punchline: Harold's assumption about her motive, which is wrong
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts parent expectations

jokes.text: MAYBE WE SHOULD TAKE A PICTURE OF THIS MOMENT, HUH?
jokes.setup: Jean saying Harold was right about something
jokes.punchline: Harold's exaggerated reaction to being correct
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Harold's rare moment of vindication

jokes.text: NOBODY CALLS ME HARRY. SHH.
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber calling him 'Big Harry'
jokes.punchline: Harold's quiet correction while accepting drink
jokes.explanation: Quiet character moment showing Harold's social discomfort

jokes.text: IF ONE MORE PERSON ASKS ME WHAT MY MAJOR IS GOING TO BE, I'M GONNA SNAP. I'M GONNA LOSE IT.
jokes.setup: Barry at the dentist party
jokes.punchline: His frustrated threat about the repetitive question
jokes.explanation: Relatable college student frustration, good character moment

jokes.text: BARTENDER'S A BASTARD! SHE KEEPS IGNORING ME. SHE'S ONLY SERVED PEOPLE WITH STUPID HAIRCUTS!
jokes.setup: Nick complaining about bar service at punk club
jokes.punchline: His oblivious complaint about 'stupid haircuts' while having bleached hair
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment showing Nick's lack of self-awareness about his appearance

jokes.text: FAKAKTE! THAT'S VERY GOOD. THANKS.
jokes.setup: Barry uses Yiddish word 'fakakte'
jokes.punchline: Lindsay's appreciation of the new vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Sets up callback usage later, shows Lindsay's intellectual curiosity

jokes.text: YEAH, THAT STUFF, IT'S FAKAKTE. THAT'S THE PERFECT USE OF THAT WORD.
jokes.setup: Discussion about paranoid marijuana experiences
jokes.punchline: Lindsay using Barry's Yiddish word in context
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Lindsay absorbing new vocabulary and fitting in

jokes.text: NO, I SAID, 'LOOK AT ME, I'M THE HANDSOME, DASHING JEW.' AND PEOPLE BOUGHT IT!
jokes.setup: Barry explaining his college reinvention strategy
jokes.punchline: His proud description of his new persona and its success
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Barry's self-aware confidence and college transformation

jokes.text: YOU KNOW, FANTASY ISLAND IS ON. WE COULD WATCH THAT.
jokes.setup: Harold being sarcastic about the party toast
jokes.punchline: His suggestion to watch TV instead
jokes.explanation: Shows Harold's desire to escape the social situation, fits his character

jokes.text: IT'S CALLED A FIGURE! OH, GOSH. THESE ARTISTIC TYPES, VERY SENSITIVE, YOU KNOW.
jokes.setup: Dr. Schweiber calling Morty a dummy
jokes.punchline: Neal's correction and the doctor's patronizing response
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Neal's consistency and the adult's condescension

jokes.text: OH, GOSH. THESE ARTISTIC TYPES, VERY SENSITIVE, YOU KNOW.
jokes.setup: Neal corrects 'dummy' to 'figure'
jokes.punchline: Dr. Schweiber's patronizing comment about artists
jokes.explanation: Good adult condescension toward teenage artistic pretensions

jokes.text: LOOK AT THAT LADY OVER THERE. HER TEETH ARE SO BAD, SHE SHOULD BE A PROFESSIONAL HOCKEY PLAYER.
jokes.setup: Morty insulting the dentist party guests' teeth
jokes.punchline: The hockey player comparison for bad teeth
jokes.explanation: Classic insult comedy structure with sports reference

jokes.text: ONE'S A DOCTOR OF THE ASS AND THE OTHER'S AN ASS WHO'S A DOCTOR.
jokes.setup: What's the difference between a proctologist and dentist
jokes.punchline: The wordplay on 'ass' in different contexts
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed wordplay with perfect setup/punchline structure

jokes.text: A BRAND-NEW PAIR OF LEATHER SHOES WILL NEVER GIVE YOU AN UNNECESSARY ROOT CANAL SO HE CAN BUILD AN ADDITION ON HIS HOUSE.
jokes.setup: Difference between dentist and new shoes
jokes.punchline: Accusation of unnecessary procedures for financial gain
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that hits close to home for the audience, well-crafted insult

jokes.text: HA, NEXT THING I KNOW I'LL WAKE UP AND MY WISDOM TEETH'LL BE GONE ALONG WITH MY VIRGINITY!
jokes.setup: Morty refusing dental anesthesia
jokes.punchline: Fear of sexual assault while unconscious
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that crosses into inappropriate territory for the venue

jokes.text: NEXT THING I KNOW I'LL WAKE UP AND MY WISDOM TEETH'LL BE GONE ALONG WITH MY VIRGINITY!
jokes.setup: Morty refusing to let Dr. Schweiber work on him
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate sexual assault implication
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that shows Neal's complete breakdown through the dummy, very uncomfortable

jokes.text: BECAUSE HE'S SO STUPID HE FLUNKED OUT OF MEDICAL SCHOOL! HEY, GET YOUR HANDS OFF ME. YOU'RE OUT OF ORDER! THIS WHOLE DENTAL PRACTICE IS OUT OF ORDER!
jokes.setup: Why is Dr. Schweiber a dentist?
jokes.punchline: The insult followed by movie reference as Neal loses control
jokes.explanation: Climactic moment where Neal completely loses control, perfect character integration with movie reference

jokes.text: SEE? TOLD YOU THAT THING WOULD TAKE HIM OVER.
jokes.setup: Neal's complete breakdown with the dummy
jokes.punchline: Bill's vindication about the Magic movie parallel
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that validates Bill's earlier concern about the evil dummy

jokes.text: BECAUSE I DON'T WANNA RUIN YOUR LIFE. DAD'S CHEATING ON YOU.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Schweiber asking Neal what's wrong
jokes.punchline: Neal's blunt revelation delivered as protection
jokes.explanation: Powerful dramatic moment with dark humor in the contrast between intention and result

jokes.text: AND YOU AND MORTY ARE VERY FUNNY.
jokes.setup: Emotional mother-son moment after revelation
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Schweiber's supportive comment about the ventriloquist act
jokes.explanation: Sweet ironic moment showing mother's unconditional support after traumatic revelation

jokes.text: MY DORM IS RIGHT NEXT TO A FIELD OF COWS. MAKE A RIGHT AT THE COW, AND THERE I'LL BE.
jokes.setup: Barry giving Lindsay directions to visit him
jokes.punchline: The absurdly rural college location with cow landmarks
jokes.explanation: Charming absurdist direction-giving that contrasts with Barry's intellectual pretensions

tmdb_overview: Daniel is sentenced to audio/visual class as punishment for attempting to pull the fire alarm to get out of taking a test. Lindsay is accepted to a two-week academic summit. Nick has a new girlfriend and takes up disco dancing.
